title: Mergepath Phase 4b automated-review verdict
description: Normalized verdict object that every Phase 4b reviewer adapter (codex / claude) must emit on stdout. Also passed to `codex exec --output-schema` so the Codex CLI constrains its final message to this shape. `usage` is required for OpenAI structured-output compatibility but may be null; adapters populate it only when the CLI exposes token counts. `cli_version` is likewise required-but-nullable (#622): the model is prompted to emit it as null, and the adapter overwrites it post-hoc with the reviewer CLI's own `--version` output (never a guessed or estimated value).

verdict.description: The reviewer's merge decision. APPROVED becomes a GitHub APPROVED review (the Phase 4b substitute clearance the merge gate accepts); CHANGES_REQUESTED becomes a GitHub changes-requested review. APPROVED must not be paired with findings whose severity is required by feedback_policy (P0/P1 by default); adapters and the orchestrator reject that combination fail-closed. There is deliberately no COMMENTED/abstain value: an adapter that cannot reach one of these two decisions must exit non-zero so the orchestrator fails closed to the manual handoff rather than posting an ambiguous review.

summary.description: One to four sentences explaining the verdict. Posted as the review body.

findings.description: Per-issue findings. Empty array is valid (a clean APPROVED). Policy-required severities require CHANGES_REQUESTED; discretionary severities may accompany APPROVED as advisory observations.

severity.description: P0/P1 block clearance; P2/P3 are advisory (mirrors the Codex GitHub App priority markers).

path.description: Repo-relative file path the finding anchors to, or null for a PR-level finding.

line.description: 1-based line in the new file, or null.

usage.description: Optional adapter-populated usage metadata. Null means the reviewer CLI did not expose a reliable token count; adapters must not ask the model to guess it. STRICT-MODE CONTRACT (#632): OpenAI's structured-output validator requires `required` to list EVERY key in `properties` (this file is passed to `codex exec --output-schema`, and the first live run was rejected with invalid_json_schema while the #602 fields were omitted), so the additive cache/reasoning/cost fields are required-but-NULLABLE — an emitter without a value MUST emit the key with null, never omit it (the Codex prompt pins usage to null; only the adapters' own post-run extraction populates the fields, and only when the CLI envelope exposes them — never estimated).

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Prompt-cache write tokens when the CLI exposes them (#602). Optional + nullable; CLI-sourced only.

cache_read_input_tokens.description: Prompt-cache read tokens when the CLI exposes them (#602). Optional + nullable; CLI-sourced only.

reasoning_tokens.description: Model reasoning tokens when the CLI exposes them (#602). Optional + nullable; CLI-sourced only.

total_cost_usd.description: CLI-reported cost when exposed (e.g. the Claude print-mode envelope's total_cost_usd) (#602). Optional + nullable; a CLI-sourced figure, never a notional estimate.

cli_version.description: Reviewer CLI version string (#622). Required-but-NULLABLE per the #632 strict-mode contract (OpenAI's structured-output validator rejects a `required` key absent from `properties`): the model is prompted to emit null; the adapter overwrites it post-hoc with the reviewer CLI's own `--version` output — never a model-guessed value.
